title: Alexandrian Protocol — Introduction
statement: Alexandrian is a deterministic, content-addressed knowledge registry and settlement layer. Identity is anchored on Base, artifacts are stored on IPFS, and topology is queryable via The Graph. Every Knowledge Block has a single canonical identity — a kbHash derived from the content of its envelope using RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization and keccak256 — that is identical across all three layers and verifiable by any party without trusting a central resolver.

layer1.name: Identity and Settlement
layer1.role: Canonical KB identity, attribution DAG, atomic settlement, royalty routing. kbHash is the registry key. Economic value flows in ETH through real settleQuery transactions.

layer2.name: Artifact Storage
layer2.role: Full artifact payloads, documentation, dependency manifests. Integrity is enforced by keccak256(fetched_bytes) == artifactHash — a commitment stored in the canonical envelope on Base. Storage location is replaceable without changing KB identity.

layer3.name: Discovery and Topology
layer3.role: Settlement volume, lineage depth, unique payer count, domain clustering. Entity IDs are contentHash.toLowerCase() — deterministic and idempotent. No central resolver.

whatThisIsNot: Not a model, inference runtime, or vector database
Not a centralized memory API or knowledge endpoint
Not token-dependent — value flows in ETH from real settlements
Not mutable after registration — KBs are permanent, identity is content not location
Not dependent on a single indexer — the subgraph schema is open source and reproducible

currentStatus.m1: Live on Base mainnet. Canonical hash mechanism, on-chain registry, attribution DAG, and settlement are deployed and verified. Full test suite passing.
currentStatus.ipfs: KB-F demonstrates complete three-layer binding. Artifact pinned to IPFS with real CIDv1 stored on-chain at publish time. keccak256(fetched_bytes) == artifactHash verifiable end-to-end.
currentStatus.theGraph: Subgraph schema designed for M2. Entity IDs are deterministic from contentHash.

forwardLooking.message: We are looking forward to building the next phase of this protocol in collaboration with the grant reviewers at IPFS, The Graph, and Coinbase. The infrastructure demonstrated in M1 — deterministic identity, content-addressed artifacts, and economic settlement — is the foundation. M2 will harden artifact availability, production subgraph indexing, and SDK-level developer tooling. We believe this architecture demonstrates a real protocol-level integration of all three networks, not passive usage, and we are committed to working closely with each foundation to make that case in production.
nextSteps: IPFS: multi-provider pinning, artifact availability guarantees, verification CLI in SDK
The Graph: production subgraph deployment, settlement volume queries, lineage traversal
Base: gas benchmark publication, attribution DAG settlement proofs, curator tooling
